title: Tender Middle Eastern Jersey Royal Potatoes Flatbread
description: A malty, flaky, brunch-mood middle-eastern dish featuring jersey royal potatoes, perfect for a relaxing morning, this recipe is also ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, dum powder, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add the warm water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops of the dough circles with olive oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Arrange the sliced potatoes on one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough circle over the potatoes to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges to seal.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook the flatbreads for 3-4 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Serve the flatbreads hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
